mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the effective federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and signals accommodative policy, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The 2016 snapshot shows FEDFUNDS stable near 0.37%, consistent with an extended low-rate regime supporting equity valuations.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive returns following declines are not statistically distinguishable from baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the 3-month moving average of UNRATE rises 0.3 percentage points or more above its trailing 12-month low (a Sahm-rule-like threshold), this signals labor market deterioration consistent with recession onset, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: 3-month MA of UNRATE rises >= 0.3pp above trailing 12-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0-5.1% in April 2016, near cycle lows
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical trigger events since 2005, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive, or the signal's hit rate is not significantly better than the unconditional 65.5% positive base rate.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS accelerating month-over-month) combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a self-reinforcing consumption-employment feedback loop that is not captured by either series alone, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. If PAYEMS rises but RSAFS stalls, or vice versa, the joint signal should not predict returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM growth > 0.1% AND RSAFS MoM growth > 0.2% concurrently
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized consumption and labor gains
failure_condition: Joint-trigger 20d forward return distribution is statistically indistinguishable from the distribution when only one series meets its individual threshold (two-sample t-test p > 0.10), indicating no genuine interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) signal forward-looking consumer confidence and construction-sector demand that historically leads broader economic expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days, but only during early-cycle expansions when housing is a leading rather than lagging indicator; in late-cycle or overheated regimes the same signal can precede corrections.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month MA rises above trailing 6-month average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1089-1111k units in March 2016, moderate expansion pace
failure_condition: Sign of the relationship between HOUST momentum and forward 60d SPX returns flips (becomes negative and significant) in more than one distinct historical expansion phase, invalidating the regime-conditional direction.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in Industrial Production (INDPRO) reflects improving manufacturing and supply-side output, which mechanically feeds into near-term corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets incorporate the incremental supply-side data surprise.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO MoM change and forward 5d SPX return is below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window, indicating no short-horizon predictive content.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in retail sales growth, defined as RSAFS month-over-month growth falling below 0%, signals weakening consumer demand that historically precedes downward earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage 453438 vs revised 444254 in April 2016, showing revision volatility
regime_hint: consumer-driven expansion sensitive to demand shocks
failure_condition: Negative RSAFS growth events are followed by positive 20d SPX forward returns in more than 55% of historical instances, contradicting the hypothesized negative relationship.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is rising (FEDFUNDS increasing over 3 months) at the same time industrial production is decelerating (INDPRO growth slowing), the combination signals policy tightening into a weakening real economy, a stagflation-like interaction that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0 AND INDPRO 3-month annualized growth < 0 concurrently
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle amid supply-side slowdown
failure_condition: The joint-condition 60d forward return distribution is not significantly more negative than the distribution conditional on FEDFUNDS rising alone (t-test p > 0.10), indicating the interaction term adds no information.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI (CPIAUCSL) acceleration above 0.3% signals inflation surprise risk that raises expectations of future rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets rapidly reprice discount rates.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage 237.92 vs revised 238.08 in March 2016, low inflation environment
regime_hint: low-inflation regime vulnerable to inflation surprise repricing
failure_condition: Triggered CPI-acceleration events show 5d forward SPX returns that are positive or statistically indistinguishable from baseline (p > 0.05) across more than half of historical occurrences since 2005.
